mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at the zero lower bound compresses discount rates across the equity market, producing a sustained valuation tailwind that propels S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of rolling windows, or if rank IC of FEDFUNDS level versus forward returns is not significantly negative over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor demand and income growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or if the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When retail and food services sales (RSAFS) rise more than 1% month-over-month, it signals accelerating consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If triggered instances (RSAFS MoM > 1%) result in negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI inflation rate above 4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading month.
failure_condition: If after trigger, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or if median return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) and rising industrial production (INDPRO) creates a Goldilocks environment where supply expands without demand overheating, which strongly supports equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy alongside supply expansion
failure_condition: If the joint trigger signal does not yield a significantly higher Sharpe ratio than the individual signals, or if the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not positive and significant at the 5% level over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales are rising and unemployment is falling simultaneously, it confirms a virtuous cycle of labor income and consumption that amplifies corporate earnings growth, producing outsized positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM > 0.5% and UNRATE MoM decline > 0.1 percentage point
regime_hint: robust economic expansion with synchronized labor and demand growth
failure_condition: If triggered instances have median 20-day returns not significantly above the unconditional median, or if the hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.6 million units signal strong construction activity that supports economic growth, but in a regime of rising inflation and tightening monetary policy, high housing starts become a negative signal because they add to overheating pressures that prompt faster rate hikes, hurting equities over the quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle with rising inflation and housing overheat
failure_condition: If HOUST >1600 and CPIAUCSL YoY>3% are associated with positive median 60-day returns, or if the regime is not followed by negative returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion when industrial production is already high, further month-over-month increases in IP indicate potential overinvestment and subsequent mean-reversion, leading to negative forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month > 0.5% and INDPRO level above 100
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated industrial capacity
failure_condition: If INDPRO MoM >0.5% with level>100 results in positive median 5-day returns over a 2-year period, or if the signal's rank IC is not significantly negative.
